channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Arab leaders endorse Egypt's plan to rebuild Gaza as an alternative to Trump's proposal
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Arab leaders on Tuesday endorsed Egypt's postwar plan for the Gaza Strip that would allow its roughly 2 million Palestinians to remain, in a counterproposal to U.S. President Donald Trump’s plan to depopulate the territory and redevelop it as a beach destination.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and MOHAMMAD JAHJOUH, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Pope stable with no new respiratory crises but will sleep with ventilation mask, Vatican says
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis was in stable condition Tuesday and breathing with just the help of supplemental oxygen after respiratory crises a day earlier, but will resume using a ventilation mask at night, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Houthis designated as foreign terrorist organization, Rubio says
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio said on Tuesday that the Iran-backed Houthis in Yemen are being designated as a foreign terrorist organization.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Indigenous migrants in northern Colombia battle worsening droughts and floods
item.description: MAICAO, Colombia (AP) — Standing next to her makeshift home of scrap metal, wood and plastic tarp, 47-year-old Nelly Mengual recounts how severe flooding and winds tore off her roof a few months ago, leaving her knee-deep in water in her home.
item.dc:creator: STEVEN GRATTAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Residents flee border area as Afghan and Pakistani forces clash over crossing closure
item.description: A Pakistani official says residents have fled a border area with Afghanistan after forces from both sides clashed into the early hours of the morning.
item.dc:creator: Riaz Khan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: UAE Supreme Court upholds sentences issued in mass trial of dissidents widely criticized abroad
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — The United Arab Emirates' Federal Supreme Court rejected appeals Tuesday on behalf of dozens of dissidents convicted in a mass trial that faced widespread international criticism.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope stable, but will sleep with ventilation mask, Vatican says
item.description: Pope Francis was in stable condition Tuesday and breathing with just the help of supplemental oxygen after respiratory crises a day earlier, but will resume using a ventilation mask at night, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: James Harrison, whose blood saved 2.4M babies dies, at 88
item.description: An Australian man credited with saving 2.4 million babies through his record-breaking blood plasma donations over six decades, has died at 88, his family said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: GOP senators urge Trump to salvage negotiations with Zelensky
item.description: Republican lawmakers are urging President Trump to salvage negotiations with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky in hopes of reaching a peace deal that halts Russian encroachment and possibly gives the United States access to Ukraine’s rich mineral wealth.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, The Hill

item.title: EU ponders 800 billion euro plan to beef up defenses to counter possible US disengagement
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — The chief of the European Union's executive on Tuesday proposed an 800 billion-euro ($841 billion) plan to beef up the defenses of EU nations, aiming to lessen the impact of potential U.S.
item.dc:creator: RAF CASERT, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump’s threats against Canada boost Trudeau's Liberal Party in the polls as new tariffs set to begin
item.description: Concern over Trump’s tariffs and annexation has found Canadians leaning Liberal with the prospect of a new leader who is set to take over from Trudeau in the next few days.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Suspect in deadly German car-ramming gives no information on motive and is ordered held in custody
item.description: Investigators say the suspect in the car-ramming in the German city of Mannheim that killed two people has given no information about his motive.
item.dc:creator: Stefanie Dazio, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The economy and AI are likely to be among the top issues for China’s legislature
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — Harnessing the power of artificial intelligence and shoring up the economy are among the issues expected to top the agenda when China’s legislature meets Wednesday in Beijing.
item.dc:creator: SIMINA MISTREANU, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Zelenskyy calls Oval Office spat with Trump 'regrettable,' says he's ready to work for Ukraine peace
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy says last week's Oval Office blowup with U_S_ counterpart Donald Trump was “regrettable,” and he adds, “It is time to make things right.”.
item.dc:creator: Samya Kullab, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Takeaways from AP's report on Greenlanders resisting Trump's talk of acquiring their homeland
item.description: NUUK, Greenland (AP) — Greenlanders have been pushed into the global spotlight in the weeks since U.S. President Donald Trump said America could take over their Arctic homeland. Most say they don’t want to be American. Many are worried and overwhelmed by the comments and the attention.
item.dc:creator: LUIS ANDRES HENAO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Syrian refugee family that Pope Francis brought to Rome prays for him as they build new life
item.description: ROME (AP) — Just before breaking the Ramadan fast on Sunday evening, Hasan Zaheda played basketball with his son in the tiny courtyard of the basement-level apartment on Rome’s outskirts where the refugee family is rebuilding their lives.
item.dc:creator: GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's trade war draws swift retaliation as Mexico, Canada and China impose tariffs on the US
item.description: President Donald Trump’s trade war against America’s three biggest trading partners drew immediate retaliation from Mexico, Canada and China.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak, Paul Wiseman And Rob Gillies, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: With the Gaza ceasefire in limbo, Israel tries to impose an alternative plan on Hamas
item.description: Israel this week introduced what it said was a new U.S. ceasefire plan — different from the one it agreed to in January — and is trying to force Hamas to accept it by imposing a siege on the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH KRAUSS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Middle East latest: Arab countries are working on a postwar plan for Gaza
item.description: Arab foreign ministers met in Cairo on Monday for talks focusing on Egypt's plan to rebuild the war-ravaged Gaza Strip, hoping to counter President Donald Trump’s proposal to expel Palestinians from the coastal enclave and take it over.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: A driver rams a car into crowd in Germany's Mannheim, leaving 2 dead and 11 injured
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — A driver rammed a car into a crowd Monday in the southwestern German city of Mannheim, and authorities said two people were killed and 11 others injured, five of them severely.
item.dc:creator: STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Iranian government official who was key to 2015 nuclear deal tenders resignation under pressure
item.description: TEHRAN, Iran (AP) — A former Iranian foreign minister who was key to the country's 2015 nuclear deal with world powers reportedly tendered his resignation Monday from the government of reformist President Masoud Pezeshkian, caving in to pressure from hard-liners.
item.dc:creator: NASSER KARIMI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Latest: Trump and his allies lash out at Ukraine’s Zelenskyy and suggest he may need to resign
item.description: President Donald Trump ’s senior aides and allies lashed out at Ukrainian leader Volodymyr Zelenskyy from Washington as he attended a European summit in London to rally international support for his military’s fight against the Russian invasion.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: UN rights chief laments 'shift in direction' in United States
item.description: GENEVA (AP) — The U.N. human rights chief has expressed concern about a “fundamental shift in direction” taking place in the United States when it comes to human rights. Volker Türk, the U.N.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Delegation of hostages released by Hamas traveling to DC to meet with Trump admin officials
item.description: A group of seven people who were freed from Hamas' captivity are traveling to Washington, D.C., to meet with officials from the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel agrees to Trump envoy’s temporary Gaza ceasefire extension proposal as first phase expires: reports
item.description: As the first phase of the temporary ceasefire in Gaza ends, the Israeli government said Saturday night it had agreed to a proposal by Trump envoy Steve Witkoff to extend it.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel supports a US proposal to extend Gaza ceasefire's first phase. But Hamas wants Phase 2
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Israel’s government said early Sunday it supports a proposal to extend the first phase of the ceasefire in Gaza through Ramadan and Passover, though Hamas has insisted on negotiating the truce’s second phase instead.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis stable a day after respiratory crisis, spent 'long periods' off noninvasive ventilator
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis remained in stable condition Saturday a day after a respiratory crisis during his battle with double pneumonia, the Vatican said. He spent long periods of the day off the noninvasive mechanical ventilation he initially needed in a sign that his lung function was improving.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Sean Penn: Zelenskyy-Trump fight shouldn't eclipse Russia's crimes
item.description: “Remember where we really stand and what this is really about,” Sean Penn implored on Friday's "CUOMO.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Tens of thousands of Palestinians seek shelter after Israeli assaults across the West Bank
item.description: JENIN, West Bank (AP) — When Israeli snipers took positions in her neighborhood, Haleemeh Zawaydeh knew her family needed to leave quickly. As the snipers' gunfire rang out, the 63-year-old matriarch said there was no time to pack as she and 14 other family members fled on foot.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, AREF TUFAHA and JALAL BWAITEL, Associated Press, NewsNation
